title: Hear Robert JohnsonтАЩs тАЬCome On in My KitchenтАЭ in Remarkably Restored Audio, Taken from a Rare Test Pressing
description:
in Music |   	May 26th, 2026  Leave a Comment
	
			

	

Robert John┬нson died at just 27 years old, some say as a con┬нse┬нquence of sell┬нing his soul
body:
in Music |   	May 26th, 2026  Leave a Comment
	
			

	

Robert John┬нson died at just 27 years old, some say as a con┬нse┬нquence of sell┬нing his soul to the dev┬нil at a cross┬нroads. But before his time came, he man┬нaged to record 29 songs, a scant body of work that nev┬нer┬нthe┬нless secured his artis┬нtic immor┬нtal┬нi┬нty as one of the most influ┬нen┬нtial blues musi┬нcians of all time. ItтАЩs unfor┬нtu┬нnate that his record┬нings, all of them made between 1936 and 1937 in less-than-ide┬нal stu┬нdio con┬нdi┬нtions even for the time, leave some┬нthing to be desired in the audio qual┬нi┬нty depart┬нment. But now, some 90 years lat┬нer, sound restor┬нer Nick Del┬нlow has been upload┬нing rel┬нa┬нtive┬нly crisp dig┬нi┬нtized тАЬtest press┬нingsтАЭ of John┬нsonтАЩs songs to YouTube: last month, for exam┬нple, we fea┬нtured one of тАЬCross Road BluesтАЭ here on Open Cul┬нture.
In the video above, youтАЩll find a sim┬нi┬нlar┬нly high┬нer-qual┬нi┬нty ver┬нsion of тАЬCome On in My Kitchen,тАЭ a song acknowl┬нedged as an ear┬нly demon┬нstra┬нtion of the young John┬нsonтАЩs oth┬нer┬нworld┬нly musi┬нcal pow┬нer. You may notice that the title labels this par┬нtic┬нu┬нlar record┬нing as тАЬtake one.тАЭ John┬нson also record┬нed a much dif┬нfer┬нent sec┬нond take, which his label Vocalion Records released in 1937, pos┬нsi┬нbly because it sound┬нed less mourn┬нful and thus тАФ accord┬нing to record-indus┬нtry log┬нic тАФ more viable as a hit.
Though take one now seems to be regard┬нed as the тАЬtrueтАЭ ren┬нdi┬нtion of the song by his seri┬нous enthu┬нsi┬нasts, the pub┬нlic did┬нnтАЩt get to hear it until 1961, when it was includ┬нed on the com┬нpi┬нla┬нtion King of the Delta Blues Singers that did more than any oth┬нer release to win John┬нson his posthu┬нmous fan base.
It is, admit┬нted┬нly, not easy to imag┬нine the first take of тАЬCome On in My KitchenтАЭ sweep┬нing the dance halls, even with this sound qual┬нi┬нty much improved from the ver┬нsion on King of the Delta Blues Singers. But the rea┬нsons John┬нsonтАЩs music has endured so long have less to do with his abil┬нi┬нty to get a crowd mov┬нing than with his com┬нbi┬нna┬нtion of under┬нstat┬нed vir┬нtu┬нos┬нi┬нty and preter┬нnat┬нur┬нal-sound┬нing abil┬нi┬нty to reach into gen┬нuine┬нly haunt┬нing emo┬нtion┬нal realms. Like many canon┬нi┬нcal singer-song┬нwrit┬нers who died young, he seems always to be and remain some┬нhow old┬нer than us, his lis┬нten┬нers, even as we reach (and indeed pass) mid┬нdle age. Occa┬нsion┬нal┬нly, the release of nev┬нer-before-heard record┬нings or press┬нings reveals the true edge of imma┬нtu┬нri┬нty in such fig┬нures; with John┬нson, it only deep┬нens his leg┬нend.
Relat┬нed con┬нtent:
A New┬нly Dis┬нcov┬нered Record┬нing Lets You Hear Delta Blues Leg┬нend Robert John┬нson in Stun┬нning Clar┬нi┬нty
Based in Seoul,┬аCol┬нin┬аMarshall┬аwrites and broad┬нcasts on cities, lan┬нguage, and cul┬нture. HeтАЩs the author of the newslet┬нter┬аBooks on Cities┬аas well as the books┬аэХЬъ╡н ьЪФьХ╜ ъ╕ИьзА┬а(No Sum┬нma┬нriz┬нing Korea) and┬аKore┬нan Newtro.┬аFol┬нlow him on the social net┬нwork for┬нmer┬нly known as Twit┬нter at┬а@colinmarshall.

title: The Myth of Sisyphus Wonderfully Animated in an Oscar-Nominated Short Film (1974)
description:
in Film |   	May 26th, 2026  Leave a Comment
	
			

	

Even if you donтАЩt know the myth by name, you know the sto┬нry. In Greek mythol┬нo┬нgy,┬аSisy┬нphus
body:
in Film |   	May 26th, 2026  Leave a Comment
	
			

	

Even if you donтАЩt know the myth by name, you know the sto┬нry. In Greek mythol┬нo┬нgy,┬аSisy┬нphus, King of Corinth, was pun┬нished тАЬfor his self-aggran┬нdiz┬нing crafti┬нness and deceit┬нful┬нness by being forced to roll an immense boul┬нder up a hill, only to watch it roll back down, repeat┬нing this action for eter┬нni┬нty.тАЭ┬аIn mod┬нern times, this┬аsto┬нry inspired Albert Camus to write тАЬThe Myth of Sisy┬нphus,тАЭ an essay where he famous┬нly intro┬нduced his con┬нcept of the тАЬabsurdтАЭ and iden┬нti┬нfied Sisy┬нphus as the absurd hero. And it pro┬нvid┬нed the cre┬нative mate┬нr┬нi┬нal for a breath┬нtak┬нing┬нly good ani┬нma┬нtion cre┬нat┬нed┬аby Mar┬нcell Jankovics in 1974. The film, notes the anno┬нta┬нtion that accom┬нpa┬нnies the ani┬нma┬нtion on YouTube, is тАЬpre┬нsent┬нed in a sin┬нgle, unbro┬нken shot, con┬нsist┬нing of a dynam┬нic line draw┬нing of Sisy┬нphus, the stone, and the moun┬нtain┬нside.тАЭ Fit┬нting┬нly, JankovicsтАЩ lit┬нtle mas┬нter┬нpiece was nom┬нi┬нnat┬нed for the Best Ani┬нmat┬нed Short Film at the 48th Acad┬нe┬нmy Awards.
If you would like to sup┬нport the mis┬нsion of Open Cul┬нture, con┬нsid┬нer mak┬нing┬аa dona┬нtion to our site. ItтАЩs hard to rely 100% on ads, and your con┬нtri┬нbu┬нtions will help us con┬нtin┬нue pro┬нvid┬нing the best free cul┬нtur┬нal and edu┬нca┬нtion┬нal mate┬нri┬нals to learn┬нers every┬нwhere. You can con┬нtribute through Pay┬нPal, Patre┬нon, and Ven┬нmo (@openculture). Thanks!

title: Who Would Be King of the United States If George Washington Had Become a Monarch?
description:
in History |   	May 25th, 2026  Leave a Comment
	
			

	

The young George Wash┬нing┬нton may nev┬нer have hacked up his fatherтАЩs cher┬нry tree and refu
body:
in History |   	May 25th, 2026  Leave a Comment
	
			

	

The young George Wash┬нing┬нton may nev┬нer have hacked up his fatherтАЩs cher┬нry tree and refused to lie about it, but his life nev┬нer┬нthe┬нless offers plen┬нty of deeds both vir┬нtu┬нous and ade┬нquate┬нly doc┬нu┬нment┬нed. It was no small thing, for instance, to refuse to seek a third term as the first Pres┬нi┬нdent of the Unit┬нed States of Amer┬нi┬нca тАФ much less to exchange that title for тАЬKing of the Unit┬нed States of Amer┬нi┬нca.тАЭ As every enthu┬нsi┬нast of Amer┬нi┬нcan his┬нto┬нry knows, this set the prece┬нdent, only once vio┬нlat┬нed and there┬нafter writ┬нten into law, of a two-term lim┬нit. But as every enthu┬нsi┬нast of alter┬нnate Amer┬нi┬нcan his┬нto┬нry has won┬нdered, what would have hap┬нpened had Wash┬нing┬нton become king? And if the U.S. monar┬нchy had remained intact for the past 227 years, who would rule it today?
In the Use┬нfulCharts video above, Matt Bak┬нer explains a series of dif┬нfer┬нent suc┬нces┬нsion sce┬нnar┬нios. While none is high┬нly plau┬нsi┬нble in itself, they togeth┬нer give an idea of the lines along which Amer┬нi┬нcan monar┬нchi┬нcal his┬нto┬нry could have played out, at least assum┬нing that every oth┬нer event played out exact┬нly the same way as it has in our real┬нi┬нty.
One of the first com┬нpli┬нcat┬нing fac┬нtors is that Wash┬нing┬нton him┬нself had no bio┬нlog┬нi┬нcal descen┬нdants. Giv┬нen that, we can trace down a the┬нo┬нret┬нi┬нcal roy┬нal lin┬нeage start┬нing with his adopt┬нed son, born from his wife MarthaтАЩs first mar┬нriage; with the nephew he select┬нed as the pri┬нma┬нry heir of his estate; or with the senior-most heir of his father (own┬нer of the notion┬нal cher┬нry tree). Not that any of those major paths through the chart of Wash┬нing┬нtonтАЩs indi┬нrect descen┬нdants is nec┬нes┬нsar┬нi┬нly straight┬нfor┬нward either.
The whole mat┬нter seems at least as com┬нpli┬нcat┬нed as fig┬нur┬нing out who would be the Roman emper┬нor if Rome had nev┬нer fall┬нen, an exer┬нcise Bak┬нer works through in anoth┬нer Use┬нfulCharts video pre┬нvi┬нous┬нly fea┬нtured here on Open Cul┬нture. The pos┬нsi┬нbil┬нi┬нties for the Amer┬нi┬нcan monarch in 2026 come down to King Robert III, or Robert E. Lee V (and great-great grand┬нson of Robert E. Lee); Queen Bryn┬нda, or Bryn┬нda Hansen; King Richard, or Richard Wash┬нing┬нton; and King Lar┬нry II, or Lawrence Shaffn┬нer, the descen┬нdant of George Wash┬нing┬нtonтАЩs nephew Bushrod. Bak┬нer finds that Shaffn┬нer is the most con┬нvinc┬нing can┬нdi┬нdate for the job, which is hard to deny. Even apart from the rel┬нe┬нvant famil┬нial, polit┬нi┬нcal, and legal fac┬нtors, con┬нsid┬нer that name again. King Lar┬нry: apart from the title, how much more Amer┬нi┬нcan could it pos┬нsi┬нbly sound?
Relat┬нed con┬нtent:
Who Would Be Emper┬нor If the Roman Empire Still Exist┬нed Today?
How George Wash┬нing┬нton Became Pres┬нi┬нdent of the Unit┬нed States: It Was Weird┬нer Than You Think

What We Can Learn from Past Pres┬нi┬нdents

Based in Seoul,┬аCol┬нin┬аMarshall┬аwrites and broad┬нcasts on cities, lan┬нguage, and cul┬нture. HeтАЩs the author of the newslet┬нter┬аBooks on Cities┬аas well as the books┬аэХЬъ╡н ьЪФьХ╜ ъ╕ИьзА┬а(No Sum┬нma┬нriz┬нing Korea) and┬аKore┬нan Newtro.┬аFol┬нlow him on the social net┬нwork for┬нmer┬нly known as Twit┬нter at┬а@colinmarshall.

title: Revisit Daily Life in China in 1917 Through Footage Enhanced and Colorized by AI
description:
in Film, Travel |   	May 25th, 2026  Leave a Comment
	
			

	

Even for Amer┬нi┬нcans, keep┬нing up with the geopo┬нlit┬нi┬нcal entan┬нgle┬нments of the Uni
body:
in Film, Travel |   	May 25th, 2026  Leave a Comment
	
			

	

Even for Amer┬нi┬нcans, keep┬нing up with the geopo┬нlit┬нi┬нcal entan┬нgle┬нments of the Unit┬нed States has nev┬нer been an easy task. More than a cen┬нtu┬нry ago, just a few months after their coun┬нtry got involved in whatтАЩs now known as World War I, they got word that the mil┬нi┬нtary of a dis┬нtant nation had joined their side: Chi┬нna, whose image would have been both opaque and for┬нbid┬нding┬нly vast. A dozen years before┬аtheyтАЩd even heard the name Pearl S. Buck,┬аwhat impres┬нsions of that coun┬нtry they had would have come from scat┬нtered sources like post-Opi┬нum Wars mis┬нsion┬нary pub┬нli┬нca┬нtions, news┬нpa┬нper cov┬нer┬нage of com┬нpli┬нcat┬нed events like┬аthe Box┬нer Rebel┬нlion and the fall of the Qing dynasty, and silent-film genre stereo┬нtypes. (Per┬нhaps the rare read┬нer got ahold of John Thom┬нsonтАЩs Through Chi┬нna with a Cam┬нera.) Most could live a life┬нtime with┬нout a glimpse of тАЬthe real Chi┬нna.тАЭ
By the end of 1917, how┬нev┬нer, тАЬthere were at least 10 doc┬нu┬нmen┬нtaries avail┬нable to sat┬нis┬нfy curios┬нi┬нty about AmericaтАЩs new ally in the Far East,тАЭ accord┬нing to the Nation┬нal Film Preser┬нva┬нtion Foun┬нda┬нtion. Most were shorts that played along┬нside fea┬нtures, but A Trip Through Chi┬нna was dif┬нfer┬нent. At least five years in the mak┬нing, тАЬthe doc┬нu┬нmen┬нtary was the brain┬нchild of Ben┬нjamin Brod┬нsky, a wide┬нly trav┬нeled Russ┬нian-born busi┬нness┬нman who claimed to speak 11 lan┬нguages. Accord┬нing to a 1912 Mov┬нing Pic┬нture World pro┬нfile, the young entre┬нpre┬нneur had moved to Chi┬нna from San Fran┬нcis┬нco after the 1906 Earth┬нquake and set up shop as a film exhibitor. Soon, as the Amer┬нi┬нcan rep┬нre┬нsen┬нta┬нtive of Vari┬нety Film Exchange, he had a hand in dis┬нtri┬нb┬нu┬нtion and by 1909 branched into film pro┬нduc┬нtion in Shang┬нhai and Hong Kong. While jug┬нgling busi┬нness inter┬нests, he filmed his trav┬нels,тАЭ all of which took place not just before Chi┬нnaтАЩs eco┬нnom┬нic rise, but┬аbefore even the Com┬нmu┬нnist Rev┬нo┬нlu┬нtion.


	

Brod┬нsky brought 20,000 feet of neg┬нa┬нtives with him back to San Fran┬нcis┬нco, even┬нtu┬нal┬нly cut┬нting it down to ten reels, which would have run around one hour and 50 min┬нutes. Of this fea┬нture-length trav┬нel┬нogue film only cer┬нtain sec┬нtions sur┬нvive, but you can see them enhanced and col┬нorized with arti┬нfi┬нcial intel┬нli┬нgence in the video at the top of the post. (Some of an un-enhanced black-and-white print appears just above.) Bear in mind that col┬нors you see are not, of course, the col┬нors Brod┬нsky would have seen; thereтАЩs also some dis┬нcus┬нsion about whether the AI ren┬нdered cer┬нtain com┬нplex┬нions unre┬нal┬нis┬нti┬нcal┬нly dark for the regions in which he shot these scenes. For Chi┬нna is quite a diverse place, not just in region┬нal land┬нscapes, cli┬нmates, and cul┬нtures, but also in the faces of its peo┬нple: some┬нthing many West┬нern┬нers would┬нnтАЩt have guessed in the nine┬нteen-tens тАФ and for that mat┬нter, some┬нthing a fair few of them donтАЩt real┬нize even today.
Relat┬нed Con┬нtent:
Behold the Pho┬нtographs of John Thom┬нson, the First West┬нern Pho┬нtog┬нra┬нph┬нer to Trav┬нel Wide┬нly Through Chi┬нna (1870s)

The Pho┬нto That Trig┬нgered ChinaтАЩs Dis┬нas┬нtrous Cul┬нtur┬нal Rev┬нo┬нlu┬нtion (1966)
Based in Seoul,┬аCol┬нin┬аMarshall┬аwrites and broad┬нcasts on cities, lan┬нguage, and cul┬нture. HeтАЩs the author of the newslet┬нter┬аBooks on Cities┬аas well as the books┬аэХЬъ╡н ьЪФьХ╜ ъ╕ИьзА┬а(No Sum┬нma┬нriz┬нing Korea) and┬аKore┬нan Newtro.┬аFol┬нlow him on the social net┬нwork for┬нmer┬нly known as Twit┬нter at┬а@colinmarshall.

title: Ava Roth Collaborates with Insects to Create 'Kintsu-Bee' Ceramic Vessels
description: In Ava RothтАЩs sculpture practice, a finished piece is the result of careful planning and tending, but the outcome can only be predicted so much. W
body:
In Ava RothтАЩs sculpture practice, a finished piece is the result of careful planning and tending, but the outcome can only be predicted so much. Whether creating wooden frameworks or organic embroideries, the artist leaves it to bees to create the ultimate form.
Roth has long invited the honeycomb-building insects to play a role in her work, often adding wonderfully bulbous constructions that occasionally disrupt the artistтАЩs carefully placed boundaries. Wooden pieces are mandala-like and take on the quality of low reliefs once the bees have done their part. Recently, she leapt into the three-dimensional realm via ceramics and a time-honored tradition of repair in her series Kintsu-Bee.

The new body of work is a play on the Japanese word kintsugi, which describes a traditional method of repairing ceramics with metallic lacquer. The process embraces the nature of the breakage itself, mending the vessel yet highlighting the cracks as a way of embracing the objectтАЩs history rather than trying to camouflage it. In RothтАЩs iteration, bees are invited to reconstruct the missing parts, guided around forms to create the missing handle of a mug or fill in the fissures of a dinner plate.
тАЬMirroring the philosophy of kintsugi, the unique architecture of the comb acts both as a restorative measure and as a visual memory of the past,тАЭ says a statement.┬атАЬWhen extracted, the delicacy and complexity of the composite objectsтАФhalf human and half insectтАФtell a story not just of human violence but of the earthтАЩs capacity for repair.тАЭ
See more on RothтАЩs Instagram.

schema.headline: Ava Roth Collaborates with Insects to Create 'Kintsu-Bee' Ceramic Vessels

title: Vibrant Miniature Dioramas by Shannon Taylor Burst from Vintage Compacts
description: From dusky woodland scenes to fantastical landscapes populated by mythical creatures, Shannon TaylorтАЩs vibrant dioramas spring to life inside vint
body:
From dusky woodland scenes to fantastical landscapes populated by mythical creatures, Shannon TaylorтАЩs vibrant dioramas spring to life inside vintage cosmetic compacts. Sourced from flea markets, vintage shops, friends, and anywhere else they might appear, the vessels themselves are often brimming with character, but they let on little about what resides inside. Tiny mermaids, unicorns, and a range of flora and fauna made with watercolor on paper beckon us into a Polly Pocket-sized realm.
In addition to the inclusion of her work in Common Waters at Arch Enemy Arts, Taylor is preparing for a solo exhibition titled Minor Mending at Modern Eden in San Francisco, which opens on August 6. She also curated a show at the Oakland Museum of California titled Fairyland @ 75: A Legacy of Magic, organized on behalf of ChildrenтАЩs Fairyland, where she is Director of Art and Restoration. The show continues through the end of the summer. See more on TaylorтАЩs Instagram.


Exterior of the diorama shown above

Exterior of the diorama shown above



A diorama in progress

title: How Betye Saar Set Black Dolls Free
description:
Features
                Nearing the occasion of her upcoming 100th birthday, an exhibition at the New York Historical celebrate
body:
Features
                Nearing the occasion of her upcoming 100th birthday, an exhibition at the New York Historical celebrates SaarтАЩs promised gift of her collection of dolls to the institution.
            
                
                                
                            
                
            
        
            Betye Saar with Black dolls from her collection, 2025 (photo Tracye Saar-Cavanaugh, courtesy the artist and Roberts Projects, Los Angeles, California)Betye Saar has been accumulating ephemera тАФ taxidermied animals, cages, computer parts, and more тАФ throughout her life. Since the 1970s, she has crafted these found objects into assemblage artworks that often subvert racist artifacts and images, beginning with her famed sculpture тАЬThe Liberation of Aunt JemimaтАЭ (1972). тАЬI knew I could not avoid the pain, so it became part of my art,тАЭ she said in 1973, as quoted in the monograph Black Doll Blues.┬аToday, her studio is filled to the brim with objects and ephemera gathered from nature, on her international travels, and at flea markets. On one special shelf sits her cherished collection of Black dolls. Some of these dolls, which Saar calls a тАЬfamily,тАЭ have traveled to New York City, where they are now on view through October 4 at the New York Historical alongside paintings, prints, and sculptures featuring figurines. Nearing the occasion of her upcoming 100th birthday, the exhibition celebrates SaarтАЩs promised gift of her collection of over 100 dolls to the institution.Some of Saar's dolls in front of a photo of her shelf at home at the New York Historical (photo Jasmine Weber/Hyperallergic)While the artist, over the years, has considered cleaning out her studio, тАЬthe dolls are the one thing she can't discard,тАЭ Wendy N─Бlani E. Ikemoto, vice president and chief curator of the New York Historical and the exhibitionтАЩs co-curator, told Hyperallergic.тАЬIt is very meaningful that she's entrusting us with these dolls,тАЭ added co-curator Rebecca Klassen.Born in 1926, Saar never had a Black doll of her own to play with as a girl. She came across her first as a college student in 1949 тАФ an Amosandra doll тАФ and became enthralled by them. She began incorporating them into her art practice in the 1970s, and they have figured into her work ever since, appearing in assemblages like тАЬIndigo MercyтАЭ (1975) and the print тАЬAunt Jemima and Hoo Doo DollтАЭ (1972)."I began collecting Black dolls decades ago. Some are hand-made, others manufactured, some even represent derogatory racial stereotypes. They each have a history and I feel some even have an energy of their past life, of the little child who loved them,тАЭ Saar said in a statement to Hyperallergic.тАЬFemale Doll with Two Heads AboveтАЭ (2020), тАЬFemale Doll in Pink DressтАЭ (2020), and тАЬFemale Doll with Two HeadsтАЭ (2020) with a Janus doll and a тАЬtopsy-turvyтАЭ doll (photo Jasmine Weber/Hyperallergic)At the start of the COVID-19 pandemic, the artist found comfort in these dolls and began pulling them down from the shelf and painting them into little scenes, using watercolors, a medium she had seldom explored over her long career.┬атАЬI took solace in painting them and imagining their mystical adventures,тАЭ Saar continued.┬аDolls are ubiquitous playthings тАФ among the oldest toys in history. Simultaneously a form of textile art and an integral form of play, they also became, over time, an example of learned racism and a way to reinforce negative stereotypes through caricature. SaarтАЩs collection dates back to the 19th century, and includes many minstrel, mammy, golliwog, and тАЬtopsy-turvyтАЭ dolls, which sit alongside some of the earliest mass-produced Black dolls.┬аA mid-20th-century doll from Haiti, flanked by тАЬBlack Doll in a Mystic Sky (Green Skirt)тАЭ (2021) and тАЬAunt Jemima and Hoo Doo DollтАЭ (1972) (photo Jasmine Weber/Hyperallergic)SaarтАЩs reimagining offered these figurines a new context and a life separate from тАФ or possibly in spite of тАФ their original intentions, transforming their meaning, alchemizing negative imagery into something potent, something positive.тАЬShe is really attracted to the energy behind the dolls and this idea that they've lived very rich past lives and that they have this almost embodied memory of an energetic charge within them,тАЭ said Klassen.The exhibition at the New York Historical opens with the only doll made by SaarтАЩs hand, in 1974, тАЬHoo Doo Woman,тАЭ informed by a Haitian doll from the mid-20th century in her collection that served as a rich source of inspiration from the 1970s until today.┬аBetye Saar, тАЬ10 Dolls with Black MaskтАЭ (2022) (photo Paul Salveson, courtesy the artist and Roberts Projects, Los Angeles, California)In her watercolors and sculptures, Saar offers these dolls alternative lives beyond the parameters of their oft-derogatory origins. In her renderings of dolls drifting through celestial skies, Ikemoto explains that тАЬthere is a deeper cultural reference to the legend of the flying Africans who lift into the sky and fly away rather than submit to bondage.тАЭ In another watercolor, the doll has become тАЬgrounded, and he is self-possessed,тАЭ she adds.тАЬShe views the act of collecting and gathering and putting things together as a sort of ritual practice,тАЭ Klassen said, тАЬfinding the energy within the object and then this ultimately transforming and then releasing the object onto the world.тАЭBetye SaarтАЩs Black Dolls is on view at the New York Historical through October 4. (photo Jasmine Weber/Hyperallergic)

schema.headline: How Betye Saar Set Black Dolls Free
